Find it on CD-ROM

Jennifer LaFleur San Jose (Calif.) Mercury News January 1997
Data & DatabasesInvestigative MethodsTechnology

This article lists various CD-ROM resources available to journalists for backgrounding people, researching government budgets, analyzing crime statistics, and mapping locations.

Driving from courthouse to courthouse to dig through documents or ordering various faxes for background information can be minimized today with the many resources available on CD-ROM. Here's a wonderful reference list for CD-ROMs: DirectPhone/SelectPhone: Two/five regional discs containing both 10 million business and 90 million residential listings. SelectPhone can be searched by name, address, or telephone number.

11 Million Businesses Phone Directory: One disc containing business listings. 1.1 Million Health & Medical Industry Reference Directory: Listing everything from physicians to health clubs. The North American Facsimile Book: Contains 150,000 fax numbers. Military Personnel/Base Locator: Contains more than 128,000 service people and civilian employees. Statistical Abstract of the United States: Corresponds to the government bestseller containing a plethora of statistics.

Budget of the United States Government: Contains all parts of the budget. Regional Economic Information System: Local area economic data including personal income and employment. Crime and Justice Data: Wealth of crime and justice data from the Bureau of Justice Statistics. School District Data Book on CD-ROM: Data derived from the 1990 Census data and the National Center for Education Statistics. Toxic Release Inventory for 1987-1990: Data about what plants are emitting what.

Bureau of Transportation Statistics: Agency develops more than a dozen CD-ROMs a year including Traffic Safety Data. Mutual Funds on Disc: Analysis by Morningstar on 3,000 mutual funds. PharmAssist: Multimedia disc explaining drugs, legal and illegal. Marriage Records Series: Genealogical information covering marriages from various states. Geographic Names Information System: From the U.S. Geological Survey with about 2,000,000 place names.

Federal News Service: Access to verbatim transcripts of Congressional hearings and briefings. U.S. Code: Laws of the United States. CIA World Fact Book: Profiles of countries. Military Contracts and Procurement Locator: Chronicles military contracts and purchasing. Defense Library on Disc: International affairs, science, defense, and military law. Street Atlas USA: Street level maps of the whole United States.

Global Explorer: Great maps of the earth down to street level in cities. InfoTax CD: Nonprofit organizations summary data. Current Population Survey Utilities: Raw data from the annual survey conducted by the Census Bureau. National Election Studies: Database of basic research on voting and public opinion.
